Part of [edX code].

# edx-opaque-keys  {{ build-status }} {{ coverage-status }}

Opaque-keys provides the ability to create and introspect course and xblock identities through a
clear and consistent API. See the code's docstrings for more information.

See the [edx-platform wiki documentation] for more detail.

[edx code]: http://code.edx.org/
[edx-platform wiki documentation]: https://github.com/openedx/edx-platform/wiki/Opaque-Keys-(Locators)

functions.name: opaque_keys.tests.strategies.classdispatch
documentation:
Like `singledispatch`, this allows you to write a generic function that
with implementations that differ on type. However, unlike `singledispatch`,
this expects a class as the first argument, and dispatches on that class,
rather than expecting an instance of a class, and dispatching on the class
of the instance.

functions.name: opaque_keys.tests.strategies.fields_for_key
documentation:
A `hypothesis` strategy to generate data of the right type for the fields of this OpaqueKey.

Arguments:

: field: The name of the field to generate data for.

functions.name: opaque_keys.tests.strategies._aside_v1_exclusions
documentation:
A strategy that can be wrapped around another to exclude
strings not allowed by _join_keys_v1, and thus not allowed by
AsideDefinitionKeyV1 or AsideUsageKeyV1.

functions.name: opaque_keys.tests.strategies.instances_of_key
documentation:
A `hypothesis` strategy to generate instances of this OpaqueKey class.

Arguments:
: kwargs: Use this to override the strategy used for any of the

  : KEY_FIELDS when building this class.

functions.name: opaque_keys.tests.strategies.keys_of_type
documentation:
A `hypothesis` strategy to generate instances of this OpaqueKey KeyType.

This is only used for property-based testing.

classes.name: opaque_keys.edx.django.models.OpaqueKeyField

methods.name: opaque_keys.edx.django.models.OpaqueKeyField.validate
documentation: Validate Empty values, otherwise defer to the parent

methods.name: opaque_keys.edx.django.models.OpaqueKeyField.run_validators
documentation: Validate Empty values, otherwise defer to the parent

A django field for storing OpaqueKeys.

The baseclass will return the value from the database as a string, rather than an instance
of an OpaqueKey, leaving the application to determine which key subtype to parse the string
as.

Subclasses must specify a KEY_CLASS attribute, in which case the field will use {meth}`from_string`
to parse the key string, and will return an instance of KEY_CLASS.

classes.name: opaque_keys.edx.django.models.OpaqueKeyFieldEmptyLookupIsNull
documentation:
This overrides the default __isnull model filter to help enforce the special way
we handle null / empty values in OpaqueKeyFields.

documentation: The types that you can set into this field.

class_variables.name: opaque_keys.edx.django.models.LearningContextKeyField._pyi_private_get_type
documentation: The type that you get when you read from this field

A django Field that stores a LearningContextKey object as a string.

If you know for certain that your code will only deal with courses, use
CourseKeyField instead, but if you are writing something more generic that
could apply to any learning context (libraries, etc.), use this instead of
CourseKeyField.

modules.name: opaque_keys.edx.asides
documentation:
This file provides implementations of {class}`.AsideDefinitionKey` and {class}`.AsideUsageKey`.

{class}`.AsideUsageKeyV2` stores a {class}`.UsageKey` and an `aside_type`, and serializes as
`<usage_key>::<aside_type>`.

Likewise, {class}`.AsideDefinitionKeyV2` stores a {class}`.DefinitionKey` and an `` aside_type',
and serializes as `<definition_key>::<aside_type> ``.

{class}`.AsideUsageKeyV1` and {class}`.AsideDefinitionKeyV1` use a similar encoding strategy
as the V2 versions, but are unable to handle particular edge-cases.

See {class}`xblock.fields.BlockScope` for a description of what data definitions and usages
describe. The `AsideDefinitionKey` and `AsideUsageKey` allow :class:xblock.core.XBlockAside\`s to
store scoped data alongside the definition and usage of the particular XBlock usage that they're
commenting on.

functions.name: opaque_keys.edx.asides._encode_v1
documentation:
Encode all '::' substrings in a string (also encodes '$' so that it can
be used to mark encoded characters). This way we can use :: to separate
the two halves of an aside key.

functions.name: opaque_keys.edx.asides._decode_v1
documentation: Decode '::' and '$' characters encoded by `_encode`.

functions.name: opaque_keys.edx.asides._join_keys_v1
documentation: Join two keys into a format separable by using _split_keys_v1.

functions.name: opaque_keys.edx.asides._split_keys_v1
documentation: Split two keys out a string created by _join_keys_v1.

functions.name: opaque_keys.edx.asides._encode_v2
documentation:
Encode all ':' substrings in a string (also encodes '$' so that it can
be used to mark encoded characters). This way we can use :: to separate
the two halves of an aside key.

methods.name: opaque_keys.edx.asides.AsideDefinitionKeyV2._from_string
documentation:
Return an instance of `cls` parsed from its `serialized` form.

Args:

: cls: The {class}`OpaqueKey` subclass.
  serialized (unicode): A serialized {class}`OpaqueKey`, with namespace already removed.

Raises:
: InvalidKeyError: Should be raised if `serialized` is not a valid serialized key

  : understood by `cls`.

methods.name: opaque_keys.edx.asides.AsideDefinitionKeyV2._to_string
documentation:
Return a serialization of `self`.

This serialization should not include the namespace prefix.
A definition key for an aside.

modules.name: opaque_keys.edx.keys
documentation: OpaqueKey abstract classes for edx-platform object types (courses, definitions, usages, and assets).

classes.name: opaque_keys.edx.keys.LearningContextKey
documentation:
An {class}`opaque_keys.OpaqueKey` identifying a course, a library, a
program, a website, or some other collection of content where learning
happens.

This concept is more generic than "course."

A learning context does not necessarily have an org, course, or, run.

methods.name: opaque_keys.edx.keys.CourseKey.org
documentation: The organization that this course belongs to.

methods.name: opaque_keys.edx.keys.CourseKey.course
documentation:
The name for this course.

In old-style IDs, it's the "course" in org/course/run

methods.name: opaque_keys.edx.keys.CourseKey.run
documentation:
The run for this course.

In old-style IDs, it's the "run" in org/course/run

methods.name: opaque_keys.edx.keys.CourseKey.make_usage_key
documentation:
Return a usage key, given the given the specified block_type and block_id.

This function should not actually create any new ids, but should simply
return one that already exists.

methods.name: opaque_keys.edx.keys.CourseKey.make_asset_key
documentation:
Return an asset key, given the given the specified path.

This function should not actually create any new ids, but should simply
return one that already exists.
An {class}`opaque_keys.OpaqueKey` identifying a particular Course object.

classes.name: opaque_keys.edx.keys.UsageKeyV2

methods.name: opaque_keys.edx.keys.UsageKeyV2.context_key
documentation:
Get the learning context key (LearningContextKey) for this XBlock usage.
May be a course key, library key, or some other LearningContextKey type.

methods.name: opaque_keys.edx.keys.UsageKeyV2.definition_key
documentation:
Returns the definition key for this usage. For the newer V2 key types,
this cannot be done with the key alone, so it's necessary to ask the
key's learning context to provide the underlying definition key.

methods.name: opaque_keys.edx.keys.UsageKeyV2.map_into_course
documentation:
Implement map_into_course for API compatibility. Shouldn't be used in
new code.

An {class}`opaque_keys.OpaqueKey` identifying an XBlock used in a specific
learning context (e.g. a course).

Definition + Learning Context = Usage

UsageKeyV2 is just a subclass of UsageKey with slightly different behavior,
but not a distinct key type (same KEY_TYPE). UsageKeyV2 should be used for
new usage key types; the main differences between it and UsageKey are:

> - the .course_key property is considered deprecated for the new V2 key
>   types, and they should implement .context_key instead.
> - the .definition_key property is explicitly disabled for V2 usage keys

functions.name: opaque_keys.edx.tests.test_properties.insert
documentation:
Operation to insert a character in a string.

Arguments:
    string: The string to insert into
    index: The index to insert the character at
    character: The character to insert
composite strategies confuse pylint (because they silently provide the `draw`
argument), so we stop it from complaining about them.

functions.name: opaque_keys.edx.tests.test_properties.delete
documentation:
Operation to delete a character from a string.

Arguments:
    string: The string to delete from
    index: The index to delete the character at
    character: This argument is ignored, but exists to match the signatures
        of insert and replace.

functions.name: opaque_keys.edx.tests.test_properties.replace
documentation:
Operation to replace a character in a string.

Arguments:

    index: The index to replace the character at
    character: The character to insert

modules.name: opaque_keys.edx.block_types
documentation:
Implementations of the BlockTypeKey OpaqueKey type.

This key is designed to provide a serialized format of an block_type
that encodes the block_family as well. It isn't intended for use in XBlock
ScopeIds (that block type should be a simple string, since all uses of
ScopeIds have a class associated with them).

methods.name: opaque_keys.edx.block_types.BlockTypeKeyV1._from_string
documentation:
Return an instance of `cls` parsed from its `serialized` form.

Args:

: cls: The {class}`OpaqueKey` subclass.
  serialized (unicode): A serialized {class}`OpaqueKey`, with namespace already removed.

Raises:
: InvalidKeyError: Should be raised if `serialized` is not a valid serialized key

  : understood by `cls`.

methods.name: opaque_keys.edx.block_types.BlockTypeKeyV1._to_string
documentation:
Return a serialization of `self`.

This serialization should not include the namespace prefix.

methods.name: opaque_keys.edx.block_types.BlockTypeKeyV1._from_deprecated_string
documentation:
Return an instance of `cls` parsed from its deprecated `serialized` form.

This will be called only if {meth}`OpaqueKey.from_string` is unable to
parse a key out of `serialized`, and only if `set_deprecated_fallback` has
been called to register a fallback class.

Args:

: cls: The {class}`OpaqueKey` subclass.
  serialized (unicode): A serialized {class}`OpaqueKey`, with namespace already removed.

Raises:
: InvalidKeyError: Should be raised if `serialized` is not a valid serialized key

  : understood by `cls`.

methods.name: opaque_keys.edx.block_types.BlockTypeKeyV1._to_deprecated_string
documentation:
Return a deprecated serialization of `self`.

This will be called only if `set_deprecated_fallback` has
been called to register a fallback class, and the key being
serialized has the attribute `deprecated=True`.

This serialization should not include the namespace prefix.

A {class}`BlockTypeKey` subclass that simply stores the block_family and block_type as strings,
and serializes them separated by a ':'.

modules.name: opaque_keys
documentation:
Defines the {class}`OpaqueKey` class, to be used as the base-class for
implementing pluggable OpaqueKeys.

These keys are designed to provide a limited, forward-evolveable interface to
an application, while concealing the particulars of the serialization
formats, and allowing new serialization formats to be installed transparently.

classes.name: opaque_keys.InvalidKeyError
documentation:
Raised to indicated that a serialized key isn't valid (wasn't able to be parsed
by any available providers).

classes.name: opaque_keys.OpaqueKeyMetaclass
documentation:
Metaclass for {class}`OpaqueKey`. Sets the default value for the values in `KEY_FIELDS` to
`None`.

This serialization should not include the namespace prefix.

Return an instance of `cls` parsed from its deprecated `serialized` form.

This will be called only if {meth}`OpaqueKey.from_string` is unable to
parse a key out of `serialized`, and only if `set_deprecated_fallback` has
been called to register a fallback class.

Args:

  : understood by `cls`.

methods.name: opaque_keys.OpaqueKey._to_deprecated_string
documentation:
Return a deprecated serialization of `self`.

This will be called only if `set_deprecated_fallback` has
been called to register a fallback class, and the key being
serialized has the attribute `deprecated=True`.

This serialization should not include the namespace prefix.

methods.name: opaque_keys.OpaqueKey.__str__
documentation: Serialize this {class}`OpaqueKey`, in the form `<CANONICAL_NAMESPACE>:<value of _to_string>`.

methods.name: opaque_keys.OpaqueKey.from_string
documentation:
Return a {class}`OpaqueKey` object deserialized from
the `serialized` argument. This object will be an instance
of a subclass of the `cls` argument.

Args:

: serialized: A stringified form of a {class}`OpaqueKey`

methods.name: opaque_keys.OpaqueKey._separate_namespace

the rest of the key.

Args:

: serialized (unicode): A serialized {class}`OpaqueKey`.

Raises:
: MissingNamespace: Raised when no namespace can be

  : extracted from `serialized`.

subclasses of `cls`.

methods.name: opaque_keys.OpaqueKey.set_deprecated_fallback
documentation: Register a deprecated fallback class for this class to revert to.

methods.name: opaque_keys.OpaqueKey._checked_init
documentation:
Set all KEY_FIELDS using the contents of args and kwargs, treating
KEY_FIELDS as the arg order, and validating number and order of args.

methods.name: opaque_keys.OpaqueKey._unchecked_init
documentation: Set all kwargs as attributes.

methods.name: opaque_keys.OpaqueKey.replace
documentation:
Return: a new {class}`OpaqueKey` with `KEY_FIELDS` specified in `kwargs` replaced

: their corresponding values. Deprecation value is also preserved.

Subclasses should override this if they have required properties that aren't included in their
`KEY_FIELDS`.

methods.name: opaque_keys.OpaqueKey.__copy__
documentation: Because it's immutable, return itself

methods.name: opaque_keys.OpaqueKey.__deepcopy__
documentation: Because it's immutable, return itself

methods.name: opaque_keys.OpaqueKey._key
documentation: Returns a tuple of key fields

methods.name: opaque_keys.OpaqueKey.__len__
documentation: Return the number of characters in the serialized OpaqueKey

A base-class for implementing pluggable opaque keys. Individual key subclasses identify
particular types of resources, without specifying the actual form of the key (or
its serialization).

There are two levels of expected subclasses: Key type definitions, and key implementations

```
OpaqueKey
    |
Key type
    |
Key implementation
```

The key type base class must define the class property `KEY_TYPE`, which identifies
which `entry_point` namespace the keys implementations should be registered with.

The KeyImplementation classes must define the following:

`CANONICAL_NAMESPACE`

: Identifies the key namespace for the particular key implementation
  (when serializing). Key implementations must be registered using the
  `CANONICAL_NAMESPACE` as their entry_point name, but can also be registered
  with other names for backwards compatibility.

`KEY_FIELDS`

: A list of attribute names that will be used to establish object
  identity. Key implementation instances will compare equal iff all of
  their `KEY_FIELDS` match, and will not compare equal to instances
  of different KeyImplementation classes (even if the `KEY_FIELDS` match).
  These fields must be hashable.

`_to_string`

: Serialize the key into a unicode object. This should not include the namespace
  prefix (`CANONICAL_NAMESPACE`).

`_from_string`

: Construct an instance of this {class}`OpaqueKey` from a unicode object. The namespace
  will already have been parsed.

OpaqueKeys will not have optional constructor parameters (due to the implementation of
`KEY_FIELDS`), by default. However, an implementation class can provide a default,
as long as it passes that default to a call to `super().__init__`. If the KeyImplementation
sets the class attribute `CHECKED_INIT` to `False`, then the {class}`OpaqueKey` base
class constructor will not validate any of the `KEY_FIELDS` arguments, and will instead
just expect all `KEY_FIELDS` to be passed as `kwargs`.

{class}`OpaqueKey` objects are immutable.

Serialization of an {class}`OpaqueKey` is performed by using the {func}`unicode` builtin.
Deserialization is performed by the {meth}`from_string` method.

If it can be parsed as a version_guid with no preceding org + offering, returns a dict
with key 'version_guid' and the value,

If it can be parsed as a org + offering, returns a dict
with key 'id' and optional keys 'branch' and 'version_guid'.

Raises:

Abstract base clase for XBlock locators.

See subclasses for more detail, particularly `CourseLocator` and `BlockUsageLocator`.
`BlockLocatorBase` is another abstract base class, so don't worry that it doesn't
provide implementations for _from_string, _to_string, and version.
pylint: disable=abstract-method

documentation: Return a string representing this location.

methods.name: opaque_keys.edx.locator.CourseLocator._to_deprecated_string
documentation: Returns an 'old-style' course id, represented as 'org/course/run'

methods.name: opaque_keys.edx.locator.CourseLocator._from_deprecated_string
documentation:
Return an instance of `cls` parsed from its deprecated `serialized` form.

This will be called only if {meth}`OpaqueKey.from_string` is unable to
parse a key out of `serialized`, and only if `set_deprecated_fallback` has
been called to register a fallback class.

Args:

: cls: The {class}`OpaqueKey` subclass.
  serialized (unicode): A serialized {class}`OpaqueKey`, with namespace already removed.

Raises:
: InvalidKeyError: Should be raised if `serialized` is not a valid serialized key

  : understood by `cls`.

Examples of valid CourseLocator specifications:
 CourseLocator(version_guid=ObjectId('519665f6223ebd6980884f2b'))
 CourseLocator(org='mit.eecs', course='6.002x', run='T2_2014')
 CourseLocator(org='mit.eecs', course='6002x', run='fall_2014' branch = 'published')
 CourseLocator.from_string('course-v1:version@519665f6223ebd6980884f2b')
 CourseLocator.from_string('course-v1:mit.eecs+6002x')
 CourseLocator.from_string('course-v1:mit.eecs+6002x+branch@published')
 CourseLocator.from_string('course-v1:mit.eecs+6002x+branch@published+version@519665f6223ebd6980884f2b')

Should have at least a specific org, course, and run with optional 'branch',
or version_guid (which points to a specific version). Can contain both in which case
the persistence layer may raise exceptions if the given version != the current such version
of the course.

class_variables.name: opaque_keys.edx.locator.LibraryLocator.RUN
documentation: For backwards compatibility, LibraryLocators have a read-only 'run' property equal to this

class_variables.name: opaque_keys.edx.locator.LibraryLocator.is_course
documentation: These keys inherit from CourseKey for historical reasons but are not courses

Locates a library. Libraries are XBlock structures with a 'library' block
at their root.

Libraries are treated analogously to courses for now. Once opaque keys are
better supported, they will no longer have the 'run' property, and may no
longer conform to CourseKey but rather some more general key type.

Examples of valid LibraryLocator specifications:
 LibraryLocator(version_guid=ObjectId('519665f6223ebd6980884f2b'))
 LibraryLocator(org='UniX', library='PhysicsProbs')
 LibraryLocator.from_string('library-v1:UniX+PhysicsProbs')

version_guid is optional.

The constructor accepts 'course' as a deprecated alias for the 'library'
attribute.

branch is optional.

methods.name: opaque_keys.edx.locator.BlockUsageLocator._clean
documentation:
Should only be called on deprecated-style values

invalid should be a compiled regexp of chars to replace with '_'

methods.name: opaque_keys.edx.locator.BlockUsageLocator.clean
documentation:
Should only be called on deprecated-style values

Return value, made into a form legal for locations

methods.name: opaque_keys.edx.locator.BlockUsageLocator.clean_keeping_underscores
documentation:
Should only be called on deprecated-style values

Return value, replacing INVALID_CHARS, but not collapsing multiple '_' chars.
This for cleaning asset names, as the YouTube ID's may have underscores in them, and we need the
transcript asset name to match. In the future we may want to change the behavior of _clean.

methods.name: opaque_keys.edx.locator.BlockUsageLocator.clean_for_url_name
documentation:
Should only be called on deprecated-style values

Convert value into a format valid for location names (allows colons).

methods.name: opaque_keys.edx.locator.BlockUsageLocator.clean_for_html
documentation:
Should only be called on deprecated-style values

Convert a string into a form that's safe for use in html ids, classes, urls, etc.
Replaces all INVALID_HTML_CHARS with '_', collapses multiple '_' chars

methods.name: opaque_keys.edx.locator.BlockUsageLocator.version_agnostic
documentation:
We don't care if the locator's version is not the current head; so, avoid version conflict
by reducing info.
Returns a copy of itself without any version info.

Raises:

methods.name: opaque_keys.edx.locator.BlockUsageLocator.course_agnostic
documentation:
We only care about the locator's version not its course.
Returns a copy of itself without any course info.

Raises:
    ValueError if the block locator has no version_guid

methods.name: opaque_keys.edx.locator.BlockUsageLocator.html_id
documentation:
Return an id which can be used on an html page as an id attr of an html element.  It is currently also
persisted by some clients to identify blocks.

To make compatible with old Location object functionality. I don't believe this behavior fits at this
place, but I have no way to override. We should clearly define the purpose and restrictions of this
(e.g., I'm assuming periods are fine).

methods.name: opaque_keys.edx.locator.BlockUsageLocator._to_deprecated_string

Returns an old-style location, represented as:
i4x://org/course/category/name[@revision]  # Revision is optional

methods.name: opaque_keys.edx.locator.BlockUsageLocator._from_deprecated_string
documentation:
Return an instance of `cls` parsed from its deprecated `serialized` form.

This will be called only if {meth}`OpaqueKey.from_string` is unable to
parse a key out of `serialized`, and only if `set_deprecated_fallback` has
been called to register a fallback class.

Args:

: cls: The {class}`OpaqueKey` subclass.
  serialized (unicode): A serialized {class}`OpaqueKey`, with namespace already removed.

Raises:
: InvalidKeyError: Should be raised if `serialized` is not a valid serialized key

  : understood by `cls`.

class_variables.name: opaque_keys.edx.locator.BlockUsageLocator.DEPRECATED_TAG
documentation: to combine Locations with BlockUsageLocators

Encodes a location.

Locations address modules (aka blocks) which are definitions situated in a
course instance. Thus, a Location must identify the course and the occurrence of
the defined element in the course. Courses can be a version of an offering, the
current draft head, or the current production version.

Locators can contain both a version and a org + course + run w/ branch. The split mongo functions
may raise errors if these conflict w/ the current db state (i.e., the course's branch !=
the version_guid)

Locations can express as urls as well as dictionaries. They consist of
    package_identifier: course_guid | version_guid
    block : guid
    branch : string

BlockUsageLocators also support deprecated Location-style formatting with the following mapping:
Location(org, course, run, category, name, revision) is represented as a BlockUsageLocator with:

  - course_key = a CourseKey comprised of (org, course, run, branch=revision)

  - block_type = category

  - block_id = name

Location fields as an array in the old order with the tag.

methods.name: opaque_keys.edx.locator.BundleDefinitionLocator._check_draft_name
documentation:
Check that the draft name is valid (unambiguously not a bundle version
number).

Valid: studio_draft, foo-bar, import348975938
Invalid: 1, 15, 873452847357834

DEPRECATED: Implementation of the DefinitionKey type, for XBlock content stored in
Blockstore bundles. This was a low-level identifier used within the Open edX
system for identifying and retrieving OLX.

Example: The 'problem' type OLX file "problem324234.xml" in bundle
22222222-2222-2222-2222-222222222222, draft 'studio-draft':

    bundle-olx:22222222-2222-2222-2222-222222222222:studio-draft:problem:problem/324234.xml

This key type was deprecated along with Blockstore:
https://github.com/openedx/public-engineering/issues/238

methods.name: opaque_keys.edx.locator.LibraryLocatorV2.for_branch
documentation:
Compatibility helper.
Some code calls .for_branch(None) on course keys. By implementing this,
it improves backwards compatibility between library keys and course
keys.

A key that represents a Learning-Core-based content library.

When serialized, these keys look like:
    lib:MITx:reallyhardproblems
    lib:hogwarts:p300-potions-exercises
